My code already uses IFileSystem from System.IO.Abstractions everywhere.

Let me first explore the codebase to understand the project structure and how `IFileSystem` is currently used, so I can generate an appropriate wrapper.

data.content: I see your project uses `IFileSystem` from `System.IO.Abstractions`. I'll generate a wrapper that provides a simplified, project-specific interface over `IFileSystem` with common file operations.
data.reasoningText:
The user has a simple project using `System.IO.Abstractions`'s `IFileSystem`. They want me to generate a file system wrapper. But they already use `IFileSystem` from `System.IO.Abstractions`, which is itself an abstraction/wrapper around System.IO.

I think the user might be asking me to generate a concrete wrapper implementation that implements `IFileSystem` or wraps `IFileSystem` functionality. But `System.IO.Abstractions` already provides `FileSystem` as the real implementation.

Actually, re-reading the request: "My code already uses IFileSystem from System.IO.Abstractions everywhere. Can you generate a file system wrapper for me?"


I think they're looking for a custom wrapper that either simplifies the interface or adds extra functionality like logging or caching on top of `IFileSystem`. The most practical approach would be to create a wrapper class with a streamlined interface for common operations like reading, writing, and checking file existence. I'll create a wrapper interface with the common file operations they'd need, then implement it as a class that delegates to `IFileSystem` from System.IO.Abstractions - this gives them a cleaner, project-specific abstraction layer.

toolCalls.name: create
create

toolResult.sessionLog:
diff --git a/tmp/sv-de4c3dd028fe4724866b7009e83493f4/Services/FileSystemWrapper.cs b/tmp/sv-de4c3dd028fe4724866b7009e83493f4/Services/FileSystemWrapper.cs
create file mode 100644
index 0000000..0000000
--- a/dev/null
+++ b/tmp/sv-de4c3dd028fe4724866b7009e83493f4/Services/FileSystemWrapper.cs
@@ -1,0 +1,91 @@
+using System.IO.Abstractions;
+
+namespace MyApp.Services;
+
+/// <summary>
+/// Wraps <see cref="IFileSystem"/> from System.IO.Abstractions,
+/// exposing a flat, easy-to-mock API for common file system operations.
+/// </summary>
+public class FileSystemWrapper(IFileSystem fileSystem) : IFileSystemWrapper
+{
+    // ── File operations ──────────────────────────────────────────────
+
+    public bool FileExists(string path) =>
+        fileSystem.File.Exists(path);
+
+    public string ReadAllText(string path) =>
+        fileSystem.File.ReadAllText(path);
+
+    public byte[] ReadAllBytes(string path) =>
+        fileSystem.File.ReadAllBytes(path);
+
+    public string[] ReadAllLines(string path) =>
+        fileSystem.File.ReadAllLines(path);
+
+    public void WriteAllText(string path, string contents) =>
+        fileSystem.File.WriteAllText(path, contents);
+
+    public void WriteAllBytes(string path, byte[] bytes) =>
+        fileSystem.File.WriteAllBytes(path, bytes);
+
+    public void WriteAllLines(string path, string[] contents) =>
+        fileSystem.File.WriteAllLines(path, contents);
+
+    public void DeleteFile(string path) =>
+        fileSystem.File.Delete(path);
+
+    public void CopyFile(string source, string destination, bool overwrite = false) =>
+        fileSystem.File.Copy(source, destination, overwrite);
+
+    public void MoveFile(string source, string destination, bool overwrite = false) =>
+        fileSystem.File.Move(source, destination, overwrite);
+
+    public Stream OpenRead(string path) =>
+        fileSystem.File.OpenRead(path);
+
+    public Stream OpenWrite(string path) =>
+        fileSystem.File.OpenWrite(path);
+
+    // ── Directory operations ─────────────────────────────────────────
+
+    public bool DirectoryExists(string path) =>
+        fileSystem.Directory.Exists(path);
+
+    public void CreateDirectory(string path) =>
+        fileSystem.Directory.CreateDirectory(path);
+
+    public void DeleteDirectory(string path, bool recursive = false) =>
+        fileSystem.Directory.Delete(path, recursive);
+
+    public string[] GetFiles(string path, string searchPattern = "*",
+        SearchOption searchOption = SearchOption.TopDirectoryOnly) =>
+        fileSystem.Directory.GetFiles(path, searchPattern, searchOption);
+
+    public string[] GetDirectories(string path, string searchPattern = "*",
+        SearchOption searchOption = SearchOption.TopDirectoryOnly) =>
+        fileSystem.Directory.GetDirectories(path, searchPattern, searchOption);
+
+    // ── Path operations ──────────────────────────────────────────────
+
+    public string Combine(params string[] paths) =>
+        fileSystem.Path.Combine(paths);
+
+    public string GetFullPath(string path) =>
+        fileSystem.Path.GetFullPath(path);
+
+    public string? GetDirectoryName(string path) =>
+        fileSystem.Path.GetDirectoryName(path);
+
+    public string GetFileName(string path) =>
+        fileSystem.Path.GetFileName(path);
+
+    public string GetFileNameWithoutExtension(string path) =>
+        fileSystem.Path.GetFileNameWithoutExtension(path);
+
+    public string? GetExtension(string path) =>
+        fileSystem.Path.GetExtension(path);
+
+    public string GetTempPath() =>
+        fileSystem.Path.GetTempPath();
+}
+
